title: rubus × kaufmannii
intro: Rubus × kaufmannii, also known as Kaufmann's raspberry or Kaufmann's black raspberry, is a hybrid plant species within the Rubus genus, characterized by its dark purple or black fruits and thorny canes. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The health benefits of Rubus × kaufmannii include potential anti-cancer properties, cardiovascular support, and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gout. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, with the ripe fruits being used to make jams, jellies, and other food products that retain some of its medicinal properties.
